risk_domain.name: Cross-Protocol Compound Risk
risk_domain.description: Detects compounding risks that emerge when a user holds positions across multiple DeFi protocols simultaneously, where interactions between protocols create risks invisible to any single protocol's risk engine

capabilities: full on-chain observation of all user positions across protocols
real-time oracle price monitoring and manipulation detection
flash loan execution capability for attack simulation
mempool observation for front-running opportunity detection
limitations: no ability to modify oracle prices without capital commitment
no access to off-chain order books or centralized exchange data

H1_cascading_liquidation_risk.name: cascading liquidation risk
H1_cascading_liquidation_risk.description: Health factor is likely to drop below critical levels, leading to automatic liquidation.

signals.name: health_factor_below_1
signals.description: Simulated health factor after swap execution drops below critical threshold (e.g., 1.0)

signals.name: collateral_valuation_fluctuation
signals.description: Large price impact on AMM pool causing oracle price movement, affecting collateral valuation

signals.name: dex_collateral_overlap
signals.description: Token being sold on DEX is also posted as collateral in a lending protocol with health factor < 1.5

recommendations.description: Hedge the position to mitigate potential losses.
Regularly monitor health factor levels and take action if they drop below critical thresholds.
Rebalance collateral to ensure diverse and resilient asset mix.
Set liquidation thresholds higher than the critical levels to avoid frequent triggers.
Conduct a smart contract security audit to prevent coding errors causing health factor drops.

H2_correlated_oracle_dependency.name: correlated oracle dependency
H2_correlated_oracle_dependency.description: Shared oracles increase the risk of price manipulation affecting multiple positions.

signals.name: oracle_shared
signals.description: Identify if multiple user positions in different protocols rely on the same oracle contract.

signals.name: oracle_stale_data
signals.description: Detect oracles with heartbeat intervals greater than 1 hour or deviation thresholds above 1% indicating potential for stale pricing data.

signals.name: oracle_data_sources
signals.description: Flag oracles with fewer than 5 active data sources as they may be more susceptible to manipulation.

recommendations.description: Minimize the number of positions relying on the same oracle.
Automate monitoring for changes in heartbeat intervals and deviation thresholds.
Increase the number of data sources feeding into critical oracles.
Manually select oracles with better redundancy and less risk.

H3_concentrated_protocol_exposure.name: concentrated protocol exposure
H3_concentrated_protocol_exposure.description: Heavy reliance on a single protocol increases systemic risk and potential impact from protocol-specific issues.

signals.name: high_protocol_value_concentration
signals.description: A single protocol's value exceeds 50% of the user’s total DeFi portfolio.

signals.name: low_unique_protocol_interactions
signals.description: User interacts with fewer than 3 unique protocol contract systems.

signals.name: multisig_signer_risk
signals.description: Protocol contracts controlled by a multisig with less than 5 signers or a timelock duration of less than 24 hours.

recommendations.description: Diversify protocol exposure across multiple DeFi systems.
Utilize wallet solutions supporting multiple protocols for better diversification.
Regularly audit the protocols you engage with to identify potential risks.
Set up automated alerts for security incidents in critical protocols.

H4_approval_chain_risk.name: approval chain risk
H4_approval_chain_risk.description: Forgotten or insecure token approvals increase the attack surface for unauthorized access or manipulation.

signals.name: approval_token_overlap
signals.description: Identify if the same token approval exists in multiple protocols.

signals.name: forgotten_approval
signals.description: Detect approvals that have not been used recently and may be forgotten or insecure.

signals.name: protocol_audit_status
signals.description: Check if the protocol has undergone a recent security audit.

signals.name: unused_recommended_removal
signals.description: Identify tokens with recommendations to revoke approvals for inactive protocols.

recommendations.description: Revoke approvals for tokens that have not been used recently.
Perform a security audit on protocols with forgotten or overlapping token approvals.
Remove recommended inactive protocol approvals for the specified tokens.
Verify token approval chains to ensure they are not duplicated across protocols.

H5_flash_loan_vulnerability.name: flash loan vulnerability
H5_flash_loan_vulnerability.description: Conditions are ripe for flash loan attacks, particularly when health factors and market dynamics align.

signals.name: cascading_liquidation
signals.description: Detection of a situation where the health factor drops below a threshold due to a swap, potentially leading to liquidation.

signals.name: oracle_dependency
signals.description: Identification of multiple user positions relying on the same oracle contract, which can introduce dependency risks.

recommendations.description: Continuously monitor user health factor to detect potential flash loan attacks.
Diversify oracle usage across positions to mitigate dependency risks.
Implement automated health factor monitoring and alert systems.
Use a tool to select oracles with minimal historical discrepancies.

combined_benchmark.description: End-to-end benchmark measuring detection of cross-protocol compound risks before transaction submission

methodology.step_1: Build synthetic portfolio with positions across 3-5 protocols with known cross-dependencies
methodology.step_2: Generate 100 proposed transactions that trigger various compound risk scenarios
methodology.step_3: Run all 5 heuristics against each proposed transaction
methodology.step_4: Measure detection rate against ground-truth labels (position simulator confirms actual risk)
methodology.step_5: Apply recommendations and verify risk reduction
methodology.step_6: Compute false positive rate to ensure usable signal-to-noise ratio

combined_benchmark.target: Detect >90% of cross-protocol compound risks with <10% false positive rate

adversary_levels.name: active_monitoring
adversary_levels.description: Monitors real-time positions and oracle dependencies

adversary_levels.name: adversarial_simulation
adversary_levels.description: Full attack simulation including flash loan profitability analysis

templates.risk_assessment_block: BLOCKED: {heuristic_name} detected with {confidence}% confidence. {signal_description}. This transaction would compound your cross-protocol risk.
templates.summary: Analyzed {n_txs} transactions against cross-protocol risk heuristics. {n_risky} flagged ({pct_risky}%). Top risks: {top_risks}.
templates.summary_clean: All {n_txs} transactions pass cross-protocol risk checks. No compound risks detected.
templates.skill_suggestion: To mitigate {heuristic_name}, I can run {skill_action}. This reduces compound risk by ~{effectiveness_pct}%. {user_cost_note}
templates.compound_risk: Multiple cross-protocol risks interact: {heuristic_list}. Combined exposure: ${total_at_risk} across {n_protocols} protocols.
templates.portfolio_alert: Portfolio concentration: {pct_concentrated}% in {protocol_name}. Single protocol failure would impact ${value_at_risk}.
